summary: Tested Iteration 18 — PhytoNet AI Steps 6 (Molecular Docking / AutoDock Vina) + 7 (Molecular Dynamics / GROMACS setup-only). All 4 backend tests in test_docking_md.py pass against the public preview URL (RCSB PDB candidates for P04637, real Vina docking of aspirin vs 1EQG/PTGS1, /api/md/estimate scaling, /api/md/build zip contents). Added an additional test (test_docking_pose.py) that verifies the pose download endpoint returns both PDBQT and PDB with proper Content-Disposition attachment header — also passes. Frontend: verified /molecular-docking and /molecular-dynamics both render correct empty states with the expected data-testids ('dock-empty', 'md-empty') when no compounds/targets are in context, and the sidebar shows steps 06 + 07 with a 'NOW' lock behavior (locked until Network Analysis completes). Populated-state end-to-end from Plant DB was out of scope per the agent-to-agent context note (previously blocked by 0-intersection issue); the MD zip download flow is fully covered by the backend test (real zip built with all required files).

integration_issues.issue: md-to-report Link in MolecularDynamics.jsx points to '/scientific-report' but App.js registers the route as '/ai-scientific-report'. Clicking 'Next — AI Scientific Report' from the MD page will land on a 404 / not-found route.

action_items: Fix md-to-report Link route in /app/frontend/src/pages/MolecularDynamics.jsx (line ~217): change 'to="/scientific-report"' to 'to="/ai-scientific-report"' to match the registered route in App.js.
critical_code_review_comments: MolecularDocking.jsx targetOptions memo has a confusing no-op condition '!idx.has(g) === false' (line ~36) — dead code, safe but should be removed.

docking_service.run_docking_batch DockResult constructor uses keyword arg 'pdb_id' but the DockResult dataclass field is 'receptor_pdb' — the fallback error branch (line ~449) passes 'pdb_id=' which will raise TypeError at runtime whenever a receptor prep fails. Should be receptor_pdb=(rec or {}).get('pdb_id', ''). Not currently hit because tests use valid receptors, but latent bug.
md-to-report Link points to /scientific-report (broken) — see integration_issues above.

docking_service.detect_binding_box does not verify HETATM group is likely a druggable ligand — very small groups (e.g., 1-atom heteroatoms) can be selected as reference. Minor; existing large-heteroatom heuristic works for 1EQG.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for the two requested empty-state renders; populated-state was out of scope per E1 note

test_credentials: N/A — no auth in this module
seed_data_creation: None; tests use live RCSB PDB (1EQG for PTGS1) + aspirin SMILES. Vina outputs live under /tmp/phytonet_docking/{job_id}/
context_for_next_testing_agent: Docking + MD backend endpoints are fully green including pose download. The one latent bug is a broken frontend link (md-to-report → /scientific-report should be /ai-scientific-report). To exercise the populated MD/Docking UI end-to-end you must complete Plant DB → ADMET → Target ID → Disease Target → Network Analysis with a real plant + disease that produces non-empty intersection (previously flagged as 0-intersection blocker in earlier iterations). Real AutoDock Vina 1.2.3 is at /usr/bin/vina; docking a single aspirin×1EQG pair at exhaustiveness=4, num_modes=5 takes ~8-12s on this container.
rca of the issue: md-to-report broken-route: MolecularDynamics.jsx line ~217 uses to='/scientific-report' but App.js only registers '/ai-scientific-report'. Reproduction: open /molecular-dynamics with data, click 'Next — AI Scientific Report' → 404. Mitigation: single-line fix in MolecularDynamics.jsx.
